title: Oroya peruviana

intro: Oroya peruviana, also known as Khat or Catha edulis, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, but also found in South America. The plant has been used for centuries for its stimulant properties and is known to have several health benefits, including reducing fatigue, improving mood, and increasing alertness. The bioactive constituents of Oroya peruviana include cathinone, cathine, and catharanthine, which are responsible for its psychoactive effects. Traditionally, the plant is consumed as a herbal preparation, often in the form of chewed leaves or a tea made from the leaves, although it is worth noting that its use is regulated in many countries due to concerns about addiction and other health risks.
